posts.author: aashutoshg2007
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — open-ended SIFs are available on Coin, while interval SIFs can only be purchased through Kite via the secondary market, subject to the liquidity available in the instrument. But I am not able to find Altiva hybrid sif in kite . What is the symbol of altiva interval fund.

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Hi @Anirudha_P , Interval SIFs are trade on exchange due to their restricted redemption windows. This creates a technical limitation where the scheme’s ISIN appears in both the Mutual Fund and Equity bhavcopies, preventing it from being simultaneously offered on both Coin and Kite. Hence, open-ended SIFs are available on Coin, while interval SIFs can only be purchased through Kite via the secondary market, subject to the liquidity available in the instrument.

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Hi @VijayNair Yes, SIFs bought through Coin are held in demat form within your demat account, just like other exchange-listed securities.

posts.author: Shubhanshu1
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — @Jason_Castelino Check this out SIF360 - Discover & Compare Specialized Investment Funds (SIFs) in India - good enough website compared to all others on this particular investment class. Performance is also there.

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Hi @Shubhanshu1 , QSIF Sector Rotation Long-Short Fund - Direct Plan - Growth is avilable for investment on Coin. We’re working on it. We plan to add a dedicated SIF section to the Coin dashboard to improve discovery. For now, you can browse and invest in SIF schemes directly through the Coin app and web.

posts.author: concorde24
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Checked with Altiva for this - and here is the response https://x.com/iRadhikaGupta/status/2056274787148955669?s=20

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Hi @BB789 , As per SEBI regulations, the minimum investment threshold of ₹10 lakh for SIFs must be maintained at all times. This requirement does not applies, if the portfolio value falls below ₹10 lakh due to mark-to-market (MTM) losses. The breach is applied if the units are partially redeemed and investment in SIF falls below ₹10 lakh.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — What about if it’s due to loss on the part of the SIF?

posts.author: vsavla
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — @Deepak_Kumar_Singh When will Coin support interval SIFs? Other platforms like Groww and Kuvera have been supporting this for quite some time now. At present, we are offering open-ended SIF schemes from AMCs to ensure better liquidity and flexibility for investors. What does this mean? Will Coin not support interval SIF? Why not just show a Nudge to the investors about the redemption constraints and let them invest if they are okay with it?

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — Hi @vsavla , The SIF schemes offered by Altiva, Titanium, Magnum, and Apex are currently interval funds, which have limited liquidity and allow redemptions only twice a week. At present, we are offering open-ended SIF schemes from AMCs to ensure better liquidity and flexibility for investors. Once these AMCs launch open-ended SIF schemes, they will be made available on Coin for both purchase and redemption.

posts.author: vsavla
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — @Deepak_Kumar_Singh - Altiva, Titanium, Magnum, Apex AMCs are missing in this list. When will these be available?

posts.author: Deepak_Kumar_Singh
posts.text: [Beta] SIFs (Specialized Investment Funds) are now live on Coin — SIFs were introduced by SEBI in late 2024 to create a new category between mutual funds and PMS/AIF products. The regulatory framework was formally released in February 2025 and became effective from April 2025, enabling AMCs to launch India’s first SIF offerings for investors seeking advanced investment strategies. Specialized Investment Funds (SIFs) are built for HNIs and informed investors who can take higher-risk investment strategies and can invest a minimum corpus of ₹10 lakh. SIFs are designed to bridge the gap between tr…
